params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: shipid
params.description:
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: days
params.description: The number of days, starting from the time of request and going backwards, for which the response should look for position data </br></br> Maximum value is 190 (days)

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Use with todate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: todate
params.description: Use with fromdate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: period
params.description:
Limit position per vessel. Omit to get all the available positions

Values are:
  • hourly: get one position per hour
  • daily: get one position per day

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also weather data

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Get all historical positions of a single vessel for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Default resolution for returned positions is up to 2 minutes. Use "period" parameter to limit the frequency of positions
  • Weather data is returned once within each hour's results
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** speed

info.name: Vessel Historical Positions in an Area

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: days
params.description: The number of days, starting from the time of request and going backwards, for which the response should look for position data </br></br> Maximum value is 190 (days)

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Use with todate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: todate
params.description: Use with fromdate instead of days to get data for a date period

params.name: MINLAT
params.description:
Use with MAXLAT, MINLON, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.name: MAXLAT
params.description:
Use with MINLAT, MINLON, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.name: MINLON
params.description:
Use with MINLAT, MAXLAT, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.name: MAXLON

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

params.description:

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: period
params.description:
Limit position per vessel. Omit to get all the available positions

Values are:
  • hourly: get one position per hour
  • daily: get one position per day

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also weather data

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:

  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Get vessel historical positions for a single or for all vessels inside an area of your preference for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Default resolution for returned positions is up to 2 minutes. Use "period" parameter to limit the frequency of positions
  • Weather data is returned once within each hour's results

info.name: Vessel Positions of a Static Fleet

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 8 to get the latest

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned positions.  </br></br> Maximum value for terrestrial coverage is 60. Maximum value for satellite coverage is 180

params.name: shiptype
params.description:
Filter data by vessel type:
  • 2: Fishing
  • 4: High Speed Craf
  • 6: Passenger
  • 7: Cargo
  • 8: Tanker

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
  • full
If used with the value extended or full, the response includes scheduled static and voyage related vessel data report (AIS Message 5). In this case your request frequency might be limited (depending on your service terms)

If omitted, the returned records include only position reports (AIS Messages 1, 2, 3/ 18, 19)

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Keep a close eye on the vessel or group of vessels of your interest.

Notes
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** returned in (knots x10) and **TIMESTAMP** in UTC
  • **HEADING** for SAR Aircrafts contains the Altitude of the aircraft (“STATUS” = 97)
  • **HEADING** values -1 or 511 indicate lack of data
    • <

info.name: Vessel Positions in a Custom Area

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 1 to get the latest

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned positions. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: shiptype_generic
params.description: Filter vessels based on generic vessel types, comma separated values supported </br></br> Vessel type ID should be used and not vessel type name (2=Fishing / 4=High Speed Craft / 6=Passenger / 7=Cargo / 8=Tanker) </br></br> Should not be combined with shiptype_detailed filter

params.name: shiptype_detailed
params.description:
Filter vessels based on detailed vessel types, comma separated values supported

Vessel type ID should be used and not vessel type name - more

Should not be combined with shiptype_generic filter

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
  • full
If used with the value extended or full, the response includes scheduled static and voyage related vessel data report (AIS Message 5). In this case your request frequency might be limited (depending on your service terms)

If omitted, the returned records include only position reports (AIS Messages 1, 2, 3/ 18, 19)

  • csv

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 6 to get the latest

params.name: shipid
params.description:
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: movetype
params.description: Use 0 to only receive arrivals or 1 to only receive departures. If not used, the response will include both

params.name: exclude_intransit
params.description: Use 1 to exclude vessels in transit

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Portcalls between fromdate and todate. Maximum period requested 190 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM

params.name: todate
params.description: Portcalls between fromdate and todate. Maximum period requested 190 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM

params.name: dwt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: dwt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: market
params.description: Define market(s) for which you would like to receive port calls (single or comma separated values) - more

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

params.description:

  • csv

Get event based intelligence for a vessel.

Notes
  • Here is a COMPLETE LIST OF EVENT TYPES available for this endpoint. You can use these as values for the **event_type** query parameter.
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: shipid
params.description:
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or greater than provided fromdate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

params.name: todate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or less than provided todate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

params.name: market
params.description:
Define market for which you would like to receive berth calls - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market

params.name: shipclass
params.description: Define ship class of interest - more

params.name: dwt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: dwt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: gt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: movetype
params.description:
Use 0 to include both, 1 to only receive dockings or 2 for only undockings within the defined period. If not used, only dockings are returned

Available only from version 2 of the service

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: portid
params.description:
The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to monitor (found on the URL of the respective Port page)

You can instead use port_unlocode, berthid or terminalid

params.name: port_unlocode
params.description: Port UN LOCODE (without gaps or spaces)

params.name: berthid
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the berth you wish to monitor (list to be provided upon request)

params.name: terminalid
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the terminal you wish to monitor (list to be provided upon request)

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or greater than provided fromdate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

params.name: todate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or less than provided todate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

params.name: market
params.description:
Define market for which you would like to receive berth calls - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market

params.name: shipclass
params.description: Define ship class of interest - more

params.name: dwt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: dwt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: gt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: movetype
params.description:
Use 0 to include both, 1 to only receive dockings or 2 for only undockings within the defined period. If not used, only dockings are returned

Available only from version 2 of the service

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

items.docs:
Retrieve arrival and departure information at a specific port, berth or terminal, for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Data is available for dates **after** 2017-07-03
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • More information about input parameters:

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: vessel_id
params.description:
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) or the IMO number of the vessel

You can instead use shipid

params.name: shipid
params.description: A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Retrieve the most popular photo of a vessel.

Notes
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 5 to get the latest

params.name: mmsi
params.description:
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track (single or comma separated values)

You can instead use imo or shipid

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track (single or comma separated values)

params.name: shipid
params.description: A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel (single or comma separated values)

params.name: timespan
params.description: The timespan parameter defines the period of time, during which we want to receive the change in a vessel's details </br></br> The maximum value can be either 5 when the interval parameter is set to days or 2880 when the interval is set to minutes

params.name: interval
params.description: The interval parameter can be either minutes or days

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

params.name: page
params.description: Page number of the results to be returned

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended

items.docs:

A new and improved version is now available, offering enhanced data quality, latest features and better performance. We strongly recommend switching to the latest version, which you can explore here: Vessel Particulars & Ownership.

You can instead use mmsi or imo

The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

NOTE: Using MMSI may potentially return multiple records as multiple vessels might have transponded the same MMSI

The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

NOTE: Using IMO may potentially return multiple records as multiple vessels might have transponded the same IMO

Notes
In case of multiple results:

  • exact matches are always first on the returned list

  • Only active vessels are returned

params.name: speed_calc
params.description:
The Speed of the Vessel that MarineTraffic will use to produce the ETA value
  • if not defined the last average speed of the Vessel will be used
  • if no average speed exists then a default value of 12(kn) will be used

  • simple

items.docs:
Get ETA and voyage information for a vessel of your choice, to any port you define.

Notes
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 4 to get the latest

params.name: portid
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the port (found on the URL of the respective Port page)

params.name: timespan
params.description: Number of days in the future to look for expected arrivals, starting from the time the API call is requested </br></br> Maximum value is 40 days </br></br> If neither this nor the fromdate/todate parameters are used, the response only includes vessels currently in port (or the country’s ports) and the last vessel's signal is received during the last 2 days

params.name: days_last_signal
params.description: Include vessels currently in port that have transmitted a position within the last x days (based on their last signal timestamp). </br></br> This parameter only works when no other timeframe is defined.

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Timestamp in the future to start looking for expected arrivals. If used, then you should also define the todate parameter </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

params.name: todate
params.description: Timestamp in the future to start looking for expected arrivals. If used, then you should also define the fromdate parameter </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

params.name: fromportid
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the previous port (found on the URL of the respective Port page) or previous port UNLOCODE

params.name: shiptype
params.description:
Filter data by vessel type:
  • 2: Fishing
  • 4: High Speed Craf
  • 6: Passenger
  • 7: Cargo
  • 8: Tanker

params.name: dwt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8). If used, then you should also define the dwt_max parameter

params.name: dwt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8). If used, then you should also define the dwt_min parameter

params.name: gt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels. If used, then you should also define the gt_max parameter

params.name: gt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels. If used, then you should also define the gt_min parameter

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, apart from the ETA information, the response includes main vessel particulars. If omitted, the returned records include only position and ETA reports

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

Notes

  • Use either **TIMESPAN** or **FROMDATE/TODATE** in order to look for expected arrivals in a specific date range
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your

params.name: portid
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to receive probable visits (found on the URL of the respective Port page)

params.name: market
params.description:
Define market for which you would like to receive probable visits - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • If defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market

Notes
  • More information about input parameters: MARKET, SHIPCLASS

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: portid
params.description:
The MarineTraffic ID of the port for which you wish to retrieve congestion metrics (found on the URL of the respective Port page).

If you wish to retrieve congestion metrics for all ports, instead of portid use agg_port=1

params.name: year
params.description: Year of interest (data available back to 1 year). Information further back can be requested ad-hoc

params.name: week
params.description: Week of Year that is of interest (e.g. for week 18/2018 input 18). For range of weeks use comma e.g. for weeks 30 to 35 (30,35)

params.name: market
params.description:
Define market for which you would like to receive port congestion - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then figures for ALL markets/ship-class
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, returns based on ship-class for ALL applicable to size class markets
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, returns figures for the specific market (no size class breakdown)


If you wish to retrieve congestion metrics for all markets, instead of market use agg_market query parameter (=0 or =1)

params.name: shipclass
params.description:
Define ship class of interest - more

If you wish to retrieve congestion metrics for all ship classes, instead of shipclass use agg_shipclass query parameter (=0 or =1)

params.name: agg_port
params.description:
**If used, portid should not be in request**
  • when agg_port = 1 is used, aggregation result of all ports are returned
  • when agg_port = 1 is used, agg_market cannot be 1

params.name: agg_market
params.description:
**If used, market should not be in request**
  • if agg_market = 1, aggregation result of all markets will be returned
  • if 0, a breakdown of markets will be returned

params.name: agg_shipclass
params.description:
**If used, shipclass should not be in request**
  • if agg_shipclass = 1, aggregation result of all classes will be returned
  • if 0, a breakdown of classes will be returned

params.name: time_anch
params.description:
The median number of days spent at anchorage by the selected market/shipclass. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if extended version of the API is used

params.name: time_port
params.description:
The median number of days spent at port by the selected market/shipclass. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if extended version of the API is used

params.name: vessels
params.description:
Number of vessels that were used in the median calculations. Value of parameter indicates greater than relation

Available only if extended version of the API is used

params.name: calls
params.description:
Number of individual calls that were used in the median calculations (e.g. if the same vessel has called twice within the same week, it will count twice in calls and once in vessels. Value of parameter indicates greater than relation

Available only if extended version of the API is used

params.name: time_anch_stdev
params.description:
The standard deviation of days spent at anchorage the previous week. Values indicate in between set to be returned (time_anch_stdev:0.5,1.5)

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if full version of the API is used

params.name: time_anch_diff
params.description:
The week-to-week difference of number of days spent at anchorage. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if full version of the API is used

params.name: time_anch_diff_perc
params.description:
The week-to-week difference of number of days spent at anchorage as a percentage. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if full version of the API is used

params.name: time_port_stdev
params.description:
The standard deviation of days spent at port the previous week. Values indicate in between set to be returned (time_port_stdev:0.5,1.5)

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if full version of the API is used

params.name: time_port_diff
params.description:
The week-to-week difference of number of days spent at port. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if full version of the API is used

params.name: time_port_diff_perc
params.description:
The week-to-week difference of number of days spent at port as a percentage. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if full version of the API is used

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

You can instead use imo or shipid

params.description: A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

params.name: port_target_id
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to put as target port (found on the URL of the respective Port page) or port UN/LOCODE

params.name: includealternatives
params.description: Use 1 to search for all available routes regarding the specific journey or 0 to not include alternative routes

params.name: includeinland
params.description: Use 1 in order to receive routes which include inland waterways or 0 to not include alternative routes

params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also route/waypoints as Linestring Geometry in WKT - Well-Known Text

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: port_start_id
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to put as starting point (found on the URL of the respective Port page) or port UN/LOCODE

params.name: LAT
params.description:
The latitude of the starting point

It can be used together with LON instead of port_start_id

params.name: LON
params.description:
The longitude of the starting point

It can be used together with LAT instead of port_start_id

params.name: port_target_id
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to put as target port (found on the URL of the respective Port page) or port UN/LOCODE

params.description: Use 1 to search for all available routes regarding the specific journey or 0 to not include alternative routes

params.name: includeinland
params.description: Use 1 in order to receive routes which include inland waterways or 0 to not include alternative routes

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also route/waypoints as Linestring Geometry in WKT - Well-Known Text

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
A newer version of this endpoint is available here: https://developers.kpler.com/spec/c78f79cf-4169-4a59-987d-e5970ea170f9

Receive a list of available routes and distances from point or port to a specific port.

Notes
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.

info.name: Reverse Geocoding of a Single Point

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: coordinates
params.description: A latitude/longitude pair that you wish to receive the MarineTraffic areas/assets that intersects with

params.name: radius
params.description: Maximum distance (in NM) from the given point that should be searched for nearby areas/assets </br></br> Cannot be greater than 10NM

params.name: exclude_ports
params.description: Flag that indicates if Ports information will be excluded from response in case given point intersects with them (0:no, 1:yes)

params.name: exclude_berths
params.description: Flag that indicates if Berths information will be excluded from response in case given point intersects with them (0:no, 1:yes)

params.name: exclude_terminals
params.description: Flag that indicates if Terminals information will be excluded from response in case given point intersects with them (0:no, 1:yes)

params.name: exclude_standard_areas
params.description: Flag that indicates if Standard Areas information will be excluded from response in case given point intersects with them (0:no, 1:yes)

params.name: exclude_custom_areas
params.description: Flag that indicates if Custom Areas information will be excluded from response in case given point intersects with them (0:no, 1:yes)

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

items.docs:
Get a list of geometries that a given point of coordinates intersects with.

Notes
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: mmsi
params.description:
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to handle, comma separated values supported

You can instead use imo or shipid

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to handle, comma separated values supported

params.name: shipid
params.description: A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel, comma separated values supported

params.name: fleet_id
params.description: If used, vessel will be added to the specific fleet, otherwise default fleet is used

params.name: active
params.description:
Possible values are:
  • 0 to set to inactive
  • 1 to activate
  • 2 to enable satellite coverage

params.name: delete
params.description: Set this parameter to 1 to delete a vessel from your fleet

items.docs: Manage your MarineTraffic fleet. </br></br> To **create a new item** in a fleet, use the active parameter. If the item already exists, it will **update** item's status. </br></br> The active parameter can have values of 0, 1, 2 which corresponds to the three states of the fleet items (inactive, active, sat enabled) </br></br> The **delete** parameter must have a value of 1 and it **removes** the entry from the fleet. </br></br> If MMSI, IMO and SHIP ID are passed, the MMSI takes precedence and t

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 2 to get the latest

params.name: fleet_id
params.description: If used, will return specific fleet otherwise default fleet is used

params.name: page
params.description: Specifies the number of the results page to return. Use this parameter to navigate through paginated results (with version 2) when the fleet contains more than 100 vessels.

items.docs:
Import a passage plan for one of your managed vessels.

Notes
  • Passage plans can only be imported for vessels that belong to user's managed vessels (see Fleet Operations solution for more info)
  • Accepted file formats are CSV, RTZ or RT3
  • Maximum file size supported is 20MB
  • File should contain at least 2 waypoints
  • Imported passage plans over
